title: Thottea dalzellii
intro: Thottea dalzellii is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly found in the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thottea dalzellii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Thottea dalzellii is often prepared as a decoction or a powder to treat respiratory problems, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create various formulations, such as cough syrups and expectorant tablets.
taxonomy: Thottea dalzellii is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Thottea, which is a group of evergreen shrubs and small trees. Thottea dalzellii is further classified as a species of the subfamily Didymocarpoideae, which is a subfamily of Gesneriaceae. Its classification is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies.
uses: Thottea dalzellii is reported to have medicinal properties, but specific information is limited.
